slides.title: The quiet *anti-inflammatory* in your spice drawer
slides.sub: Gingerol is the active compound that gives ginger its bite. It also calms the body in some surprising ways.

slides.title: Meet gingerol
slides.body: Gingerol is the main bioactive compound in fresh ginger. It is *what gives ginger* its warmth, its sharpness and most of its effects.

slides.title: It cools the fire
slides.body: Gingerol can dial down the COX and prostaglandin pathways your body uses to *drive inflammation*, the same targets many pain relievers act on.

slides.title: Easier joints
slides.body: By quieting those signals, ginger is linked to *less joint pain* and stiffness, which is why it shows up in studies on osteoarthritis.

slides.title: A calmer stomach
slides.body: Ginger is one of the best studied natural remedies for nausea, and small servings *may help* steady blood sugar after a meal.

slides.title: Fresh, daily, small
slides.body: A few grams of fresh ginger in tea, stir fry or a smoothie is plenty. Consistency *matters more* than any single big dose.

slides.title: See what *actually moves* your numbers.
slides.body: Add ginger to a meal and Vibe Health logs it alongside your sleep, movement and steps, then shows the impact in one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The sharp little kick in ginger is doing more than flavoring your tea. 🌿

Gingerol can calm the inflammatory pathways behind joint pain, settle a queasy stomach, and may steady post-meal blood sugar. A few grams of fresh ginger a day is all it takes. Save this for your next grocery run. 🔖

Vibe Health turns the small things you eat and do into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Gingerol: The Anti-Inflammatory Compound in Ginger
